text:
In the heart of autumn, in a cozy little town, lived a wise old owl named Oliver. It wasn't just any ordinary town; it was home to a strange phenomenon the locals whispered about but nobody dared to ask directly. Every autumn, just before nighttime, the leaves began to shimmer in an otherworldly golden glow, as if the woods were receiving a mysterious, unseen gift.

Oliver loved this time of year. The magic of falling leaves fascinated him. He'd stretch his wings slowly, feeling the crisp air and the anticipation of the upcoming events. One particular autumn day, he decided to invite Sara, a curious little squirrel he'd grown fond of.

Sara skittered closer, her eyes wide with wonder. Oliver had never shared his secret before.

"It's a custom," he said, his voice soft. "The forest has received a gift from the moon—the leaves shimmer."

Sara stared at the leaves, mesmerized. "But why?"

Oliver chuckled. "
